result.summary: Mapped Box’s public developer integration surface: 1 REST API, 1 hosted MCP server, and 1 CLI; all require Box-issued credentials (bearer access tokens, plus OAuth/JWT/CCG app credentials depending on surface/setup).

box_access_token.label: Box access token
box_access_token.setup: Create a Box app or use the app you already have in the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console). For quick testing, open the app’s **Configuration** tab and choose **Generate Developer Token** as shown in [Developer Tokens](https://developer.box.com/guides/authentication/tokens/developer-tokens). For production auth, configure one of Box’s supported auth methods from the [Authentication overview](https://developer.box.com/guides/authentication) and mint an access token for your app/user. Send the resulting token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

box_oauth_app.label: Box OAuth client credentials
box_oauth_app.setup: In the [Developer Console](https://cloud.app.box.com/developers/console), create or open an application. For a standard OAuth app, in **Configuration** make sure the app uses **Standard OAuth 2.0**, set the redirect URI required by your client (for the CLI docs, Box uses `http://localhost:3000/callback`; for MCP use the redirect URI provided by your MCP client), choose scopes, save, and copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**. Box documents this in the [CLI guide](https://developer.box.com/guides/cli) and [MCP setup guide](https://developer.box.com/guides/box-mcp/setup).

box_ccg_app.label: Box client-credentials app
box_ccg_app.setup: In the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console), create a **Platform Application** using **Server Authentication (with Client Credentials Grant)** as described in [Client Credentials Grant](https://developer.box.com/guides/authentication/client-credentials). Ensure the app is authorized for your enterprise and copy the app’s **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from **Configuration**. Use them to obtain Box access tokens from the token endpoint, then call APIs with the resulting bearer token.

box_jwt_app.label: Box JWT app credentials
box_jwt_app.setup: In the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console), create a **Platform Application** with **JWT Auth** as described in [JWT Auth](https://developer.box.com/guides/authentication/jwt). Configure the app, generate/download its key pair in the app configuration, and authorize it for your enterprise. Use the JWT app configuration plus key pair to obtain Box access tokens server-to-server, then call APIs with the resulting bearer token.

mechanics.command: box configure:environments:add / JWT auth flow via CLI configuration

packages.runtimeHint: npx box (bundled in the `box` npm package; requires Node.js 22+)
